title: BitBrat architecture.yaml (v1)
description: Schema for the canonical BitBrat platform definition file (architecture.yaml). It is intentionally permissive (additionalProperties: true at the top level and within most blocks) so descriptive/documentation blocks can be added without breaking validation, while still asserting the core structure that the `brat` tooling relies on. This mirrors the runtime Zod ArchitectureSchema in tools/brat/src/config/schema.ts.

description.description: Short prose description of the platform.

version.description: Semantic version. Kept in sync with package.json "version".

llm_guidance.description: Guidance and invariants for LLM/agent contributors.

messaging.description: Internal event bus: contracts, conventions, and topic catalog.

conventions.description: How env vars and secrets are supplied (purpose + source, no values).

references.description: Pointers from the canonical file into the documentation/ tree.

architecture_schema.description: Path to this JSON Schema, so humans and agents can self-validate architecture.yaml.

extension_points.description: How to extend the platform with a new agent/service/MCP tool and which files change.

agents.description: Optional agent-centric extension metadata (alias surface for extension_points).

defaults.description: Defaults applied to entities unless overridden.

services.description: Map of service key -> service definition.

profile.description: Bit model: declarative capability profile. Absent => core.

mcp.description: Bit model: MCP control-plane exposure for this Bit.

exposure.description: platform-only exposes just the bit.* control plane; platform+domain also exposes the Bit's domain tools. Default platform-only.
